title: Subsets
prompt:
Given an integer array nums of unique elements, return all possible subsets (the power set).

The solution set must not contain duplicate subsets. Return the subsets in any order, but each individual subset should be sorted, and the list of subsets should be sorted lexicographically.

Example: nums = [1,2,3] → [[],[1],[1,2],[1,2,3],[1,3],[2],[2,3],[3]].
constraints: All the numbers of nums are unique

stdin.python:
result = sorted([sorted(s) for s in solve([-1,0,1])])
print(result)

notesForAI.coreIdea: Either backtracking (for each element, include or exclude it) or bit manipulation (iterate 0..2^n-1, each bit pattern represents a subset). Sort nums first to ensure consistent ordering.
commonBugs: Not sorting the input first leading to inconsistent subset orderings
Mutating the current subset list without making a copy before appending to results
Off-by-one in the recursion start index
